headline: Pulls the latest patched base-image packages at build time
summary: The container image now upgrades every base-operating-system package to its latest patched build during the image build, instead of relying solely on the base image's own rebuild cadence. This closes the window where a freshly-published operating-system security fix has reached the package repository but not yet the rebuilt base image, so the image ships with the current patched packages. No configuration changes are required.

items.title: The image upgrades its base-OS packages to the latest patched build at build time
items.body: The build now runs an explicit package upgrade against the base image so it always ships the latest patched operating-system packages (for example a just-released glibc fix), independent of when the upstream base image is next rebuilt. Previously a security fix that had landed in the package repository but not yet in a rebuilt base image would ship unpatched until the base image caught up.
